search_summary.critical_finding: cbbdata R package is actively maintained with free API access to historical Barttorvik ratings back to 2015, but NO direct Python wrapper exists

repositories.citation: [1] Weatherman, Andrew. 'cbbdata - College Basketball Data R Package.' GitHub, Last pushed: 2024-10-31. https://github.com/andreweatherman/cbbdata
key_features: Nearly 30 endpoints for college basketball data
Database updated every 15 minutes during season
Built with Flask/Python backend, SQL queries
Includes KenPom data if email matches KenPom subscription
repositories.architecture: R package frontend -> Flask API backend -> SQL database
repositories.usage_example:
# R code
library(cbbdata)
cbd_login(username='user', password='pass')

# Get historical ratings for specific date
ratings_2022 <- cbd_torvik_ratings_archive(year=2022, team='Duke')

# Filter by conference and year
acc_2023 <- cbd_torvik_ratings_archive(year=2023, conf='ACC')
limitations: R-only package - no native Python support
Last code push Oct 2024 (4 months ago)
Historical data only goes back to 2015 (need 2020-2025 covered)
No clear date parameter for daily queries (filters by year/team/conf)
Unclear if provides open/close odds or just current ratings

point_in_time_ratings.date_parameter: Filters by year, team, conf - NOT daily date string
point_in_time_ratings.granularity: Day-by-day ratings claimed but unclear how to query specific dates
point_in_time_ratings.validation_for_backtesting: UNCERTAIN - need to verify if can get 'ratings as of 2022-01-15' format

repositories.citation: [2] Weatherman, Andrew. 'toRvik - T-Rank Data R Package.' GitHub, Archived: True, Last pushed: 2024-01-06. https://github.com/andreweatherman/toRvik
repositories.architecture: R package with direct web scraping from Barttorvik.com
repositories.usage_example:
# R code (DEPRECATED)
library(toRvik)

# Get ratings for specific date
bart_archive(date='20220113')  # YYYYMMDD format

# Get ratings for team/year
bart_ratings(year=2022, team='Duke')
limitations: Should not be used for new projects

point_in_time_ratings.validation_for_backtesting: YES - explicit date parameter perfect for avoiding look-ahead bias
point_in_time_ratings.status: DEPRECATED but shows cbbdata should have similar capability

repositories.citation: [3] dcstats. 'CBBpy - Python NCAA Basketball Web Scraper.' GitHub, Last pushed: 2025-11-26. https://github.com/dcstats/CBBpy
key_features: Python-based web scraper for NCAA basketball
Game metadata (date, time, score, teams, referees)
repositories.usage_example:
# Python code
import cbbpy

s = cbbpy.Scraper()

# Get game info
info = s.get_game_info(game_id='12345')

# Get box score
boxscore = s.get_game_boxscore(game_id='12345')

# Get play-by-play
pbp = s.get_game_pbp(game_id='12345')
limitations: NO efficiency metrics (AdjO, AdjD, AdjEM, AdjT)
Only play-by-play and box score data
alternatives: Direct API requests to cbbdata Flask backend

point_in_time_ratings.method: N/A - does not provide Barttorvik ratings

repositories.citation: [4] CFBD. 'cbbd-python - College Basketball Data API Python Client.' GitHub/PyPI. https://github.com/CFBD/cbbd-python
repositories.usage_example:
# Python code
pip install cbbd

# Usage unclear from search results

common_patterns: R ecosystem dominates college basketball analytics
Free access via registration (email-based API keys)
Barttorvik.com is primary data source but no official API
Python options focus on play-by-play, not advanced metrics
best_practices: Use point-in-time ratings to avoid look-ahead bias
Verify data freshness (15-min updates during season)
Cross-reference multiple rating systems (KenPom, NET, T-Rank)
Filter garbage time from efficiency calculations
pitfalls: R-only packages require rpy2 bridge from Python
No native Python wrapper for Barttorvik ratings
Historical data varies by package (2014 vs 2015 start)
Date parameter formats inconsistent (YYYYMMDD vs year filter)
API documentation often incomplete or work-in-progress
Unclear if ratings include open/close values for CLV
emerging_trends: Free data democratization vs paid KenPom
Recency bias and garbage time filtering gaining adoption
Python sports analytics community growing but behind R

implementation_recommendations.scenario: Need Barttorvik efficiency metrics (AdjO, AdjD, AdjEM, AdjT) for 2020-2025 backtest
implementation_recommendations.recommended_solution: Option 1: Use cbbdata R package via rpy2 Python bridge
implementation_recommendations.rationale: Only free option with confirmed historical ratings back to 2015. Covers required 2020-2025 range. Active maintenance (13 issues show engagement). Database updated every 15min during season.
implementation_steps: 1. Install R and rpy2: pip install rpy2
3. Register for free API key via cbd_create_account()
7. Convert R dataframes to pandas with rpy2.robjects.pandas2ri
implementation_recommendations.code_example:
import rpy2.robjects as ro
from rpy2.robjects.packages import importr
from rpy2.robjects import pandas2ri

pandas2ri.activate()

# Import cbbdata
cbbdata = importr('cbbdata')

# Login (credentials from .Renviron)
cbbdata.cbd_login()

# Get 2022 ACC ratings
ratings_r = cbbdata.cbd_torvik_ratings_archive(year=2022, conf='ACC')
ratings_pd = pandas2ri.rpy2py(ratings_r)

# Now have pandas DataFrame with AdjO, AdjD, AdjEM, AdjT
risks: rpy2 can be fragile on Windows (PATH issues)
Unclear if cbd_torvik_ratings_archive supports daily date queries
Need to verify point-in-time data availability

implementation_recommendations.scenario: Need Barttorvik efficiency metrics (AdjO, AdjD, AdjEM, AdjT) for 2020-2025 backtest
implementation_recommendations.rationale: Official API with guaranteed point-in-time data. No R/Python bridge complexity. Proven reliability. Industry standard for betting models. Better documentation.
implementation_steps: 1. Subscribe to KenPom for $25/year (basic) or $95/year (API access)
3. Use Python requests library to fetch data
4. Store locally in SQLite for backtesting
implementation_recommendations.code_example:
import requests
import pandas as pd

API_KEY = os.getenv('KENPOM_API_KEY')
BASE_URL = 'https://api.kenpom.com/v1'

# Get ratings for specific date
response = requests.get(
    f'{BASE_URL}/ratings',
    params={'date': '2022-01-15'},
    headers={'Authorization': f'Bearer {API_KEY}'}
)

ratings = pd.DataFrame(response.json())
# Returns: team, AdjEM, AdjO, AdjD, AdjT, etc.
risks: May not justify cost if model doesn't profit

implementation_recommendations.scenario: Need Barttorvik efficiency metrics (AdjO, AdjD, AdjEM, AdjT) for 2020-2025 backtest
implementation_recommendations.recommended_solution: Option 3: Direct HTTP requests to cbbdata Flask API (if available)
implementation_recommendations.rationale: Bypasses R entirely. Native Python requests. Free access. If cbbdata backend is accessible via REST, this is cleanest solution.
implementation_steps: 2. Inspect cbbdata R package HTTP calls (network traffic)
4. Build requests to Flask API endpoints
implementation_recommendations.code_example:
import requests
import pandas as pd

BASE_URL = 'https://cbbdata.aweatherman.com/api'  # HYPOTHETICAL

# Login to get session token
login = requests.post(
    f'{BASE_URL}/login',
    json={'username': 'user', 'password': 'pass'}
)
token = login.json()['api_key']

# Get historical ratings
response = requests.get(
    f'{BASE_URL}/torvik/ratings/archive',
    params={'year': 2022, 'conf': 'ACC'},
    headers={'Authorization': f'Bearer {token}'}
)

ratings = pd.DataFrame(response.json())
risks: API endpoints may not be publicly documented
Breaking changes if API is internal-only
Violates API terms if not officially supported

implementation_recommendations.scenario: Need Barttorvik efficiency metrics (AdjO, AdjD, AdjEM, AdjT) for 2020-2025 backtest
implementation_recommendations.rationale: Last resort. No API dependency. Full control. But fragile and potentially against ToS.
implementation_steps: 6. Handle date parameter for historical queries
implementation_recommendations.code_example:
import requests
from bs4 import BeautifulSoup
import pandas as pd
import time

# Scrape specific date
url = 'https://barttorvik.com/trank.php'
params = {'date': '20220115'}  # YYYYMMDD

response = requests.get(url, params=params)
soup = BeautifulSoup(response.text, 'html.parser')

# Parse ratings table
table = soup.find('table', id='ratings-table')  # HYPOTHETICAL
ratings = pd.read_html(str(table))[0]

time.sleep(2)  # Rate limit
risks: May violate site Terms of Service
No guarantee of historical data availability

recency_bias.barttorvik: 40-day decay (1% per day until 80 days, then 60% weight)
recency_bias.kenpom: No recency weighting (equal weight all games)

metrics.both: AdjEM, AdjO, AdjD, AdjT, Four Factors, Barthag

accuracy_comparison.note: Statistical tie between KenPom and Barttorvik

correlation.differences: Mid-tier teams may diverge due to recency/garbage-time handling

barttorvik_vs_kenpom.recommendation: Barttorvik is competitive with KenPom quality at $0 cost. Try free option first, upgrade to KenPom API only if Barttorvik data quality issues emerge.

point_in_time_ratings_critical.why_critical: Sports betting backtesting REQUIRES ratings as they existed BEFORE each game to avoid look-ahead bias. Using end-of-season ratings creates artificially inflated model performance.

cbbdata_capability.documentation: INCOMPLETE - unclear if date parameter exists
cbbdata_capability.toRvik_precedent: bart_archive(date='20220113') shows it SHOULD be possible
cbbdata_capability.verification_needed: CRITICAL - must test if cbd_torvik_ratings_archive() accepts date string or only year/team/conf filters

testing_protocol: 5. Inspect returned data for 'as_of_date' or timestamp column
6. If no date parameter, may need to fetch full year and filter client-side
7. Validate data freshness (ratings should change daily during season)
fallback_if_no_date_param: Filter by date client-side in Python
Requires knowing how ratings evolve (daily snapshots vs game-by-game updates)

python_integration_paths.recommended_approach: Start with rpy2, fall back to KenPom API if rpy2 issues persist beyond 1 day of debugging

popular_solutions: Barttorvik.com web interface for manual analysis
controversial_topics: Recency bias: Does it help or hurt predictions?
Garbage time: Should it be filtered or included?
Free vs paid: Is KenPom worth $95/year?
R vs Python: Which is better for sports analytics?
expert_opinions: Barttorvik recency weighting may improve in-season predictions
KenPom historical consistency valuable for cross-season analysis
Efficiency metrics (AdjEM) superior to Elo for college basketball
Four Factors (eFG%, TO%, OR%, FTR) critical for game-level modeling

immediate_next_steps: 1. Install R (4.0+) and rpy2 in sports-betting venv
3. Register for free API key: cbd_create_account()
5. CRITICAL: Verify if date='20220115' parameter works (like old bart_archive)
6. Document exact function signature and return schema
7. Build Python wrapper function using rpy2
9. Compare Barttorvik AdjEM to existing Elo predictions
10. Run Gatekeeper validation with new features

decision_point.condition: If rpy2 setup exceeds 8 hours OR cbd_torvik_ratings_archive doesn't support daily dates
decision_point.action: Switch to KenPom API ($95/year) and move forward
decision_point.rationale: Time is money. $95 is 1.9% of $5000 bankroll. If it saves 10+ hours and provides better data quality, ROI is positive.

final_recommendation.primary_path: cbbdata R package via rpy2 (2-day max effort)
final_recommendation.expected_outcome: Free access to Barttorvik AdjO, AdjD, AdjEM, AdjT for 2020-2025 seasons with point-in-time ratings suitable for backtesting
final_recommendation.confidence_level: MEDIUM - hinges on cbd_torvik_ratings_archive() supporting daily date queries (unverified)
